steps.title: Use the perpendicularity property of tangent lines
steps.content: A tangent line to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. Since line $t$ has slope $\frac{6}{5}$, the radius from center $(2, 9)$ to point $(a, -4)$ must have slope $-\frac{5}{6}$ (the negative reciprocal).

steps.title: Solve for a using the slope equation
steps.content:
The slope of the radius connecting $(2, 9)$ to $(a, -4)$ is $\frac{-13}{a - 2}$, which must equal $-\frac{5}{6}$:
$$\frac{-13}{a - 2} = -\frac{5}{6}$$
$$-13 \cdot 6 = -5(a - 2)$$
$$-78 = -5a + 10$$
$$-88 = -5a$$
$$a = \frac{88}{5}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
